title: Applied Quantitative Genetics Lab
text:
Genetics Ecology and Conservation Forensics


Applied Quantitative Genetics Lab








Welcome
The Sauve Lab is based in the Faculty of Science (Forensic Science) at Ontario Tech University. Broadly, I use quantitative genetics and population genetics to better understand population responses to changing environments and to solve applied problems. I get excited about the use of pedigrees, studbooks, genomics, and genealogical records, and I am beginning to explore how these tools and frameworks can extend to forensics.


Research themes






    



Ecology and Conservation
I study zoological collections and wildlife populations to better understand contemporary responses to environmental change.






             



Genetics
I use sequencing and relatedness to study evolutionary processes.






      



Forensics
I am interested in understanding how genetics and statistical tools from evolutionary ecology might be applied in forensic contexts.





Lab at a glance



35 Pedigrees Analyzed


3 Team Members


16 Publications


2026 Founded





Interested in joining the lab?
We welcome motivated students and postdocs interested in evolutionary genetics, conservation, and quantitative biology.
See open positions →

title: Team
text:
Principal Investigator





Dr. Drew Sauve


Principal Investigator

Assistant Professor, Faculty of Science - Forensic Science
Ontario Tech University

CV · Email · Google Scholar



Dr. Drew Sauve is an Assistant Professor in the Faculty of Science (Forensic Science) at Ontario Tech University. His research uses quantitative genetics and statistical modeling to understand how organisms change phenotypes through evolution and plasticity. His work has focused on climate change and conservation applications, and he is beginning to explore how population genetic frameworks can address forensic questions.

The official start to the lab
We are excited and thrilled to be starting the Applied Quantitative Genetics Lab at Ontario Tech University in Oshawa! Looking forward to building the team and doing some great science!

title: Join Us
text:
Opportunities
We are always looking for motivated, collaborative, and curious people to join the Lab. We strive to be an kind, inclusive, and supportive environment for researchers at all stages.

title: Join Us
text:
PhD & MSc Students
Please email me so we can discuss your interests. I am always excited to explore funding opportunities for students interested in quantitative genetics, evolutionary ecology, conservation, and forensic genetics.
What to include in your email:

A brief description of your research interests and why you want to join this lab
Your CV, including academic background and any research experience

Graduate programs at Ontario Tech:
→ MSc in Biology
→ PhD in Biology

title: Join Us
text:
Postdoctoral Researchers
I welcome inquiries from candidates interested in developing independent fellowship applications (e.g., NSERC, Banting, Liber Ero, MITACs). If your research interests align with the lab’s work, please get in touch with a CV and a brief description of your interests.

title: Join Us
text:
Undergraduate Students
We welcome motivated undergraduates interested in gaining research experience in quantitative genetics and evolutionary biology. —

title: Join Us
text:
Contact

Get in touch
To inquire about opportunities, email me with the subject line “Prospective [PhD/MSc/PDF/Undergrad] — [Your Name]”.
I aim to respond to all serious enquiries within two weeks.
Email the lab


Ontario Tech University is located in Oshawa, Ontario, within the Greater Toronto Area. The university is committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion in all its activities. We particularly encourage applications from members of groups underrepresented in STEM.

title: Research
text:
Overview
We mainly work to understand how genetic and environmental variation combine to produce the phenotypes we see in nature. We use that understanding to predict how populations evolve, persist, or decline. We apply quantitative genetics, population genetics, and pedigree/studbook/genealogical analysis to questions across evolutionary ecology, conservation, and forensic science.

title: Research
text:
Evolutionary ecology
We are interested in understanding the genetic and environmental contributors to phenotypic variation so that we can make predictions about phenotypic and demographic change.
Key questions:

How do genotypes express phenotypes across different environmental/genetic contexts? What does this mean for conservation, responses to climate change or phenotypic prediction?
Can we predict adaptation and demographic change?
How reliably can we estimate quantitative genetic parameters from genomic data?

title: Research
text:
Wildlife conservation
We have a strong interest in making connections from basic evolutionary ecology to applied conservation biology. We work with species-at-risk to translate data and theory into conservation guidance.
Key questions:

How can we use molecular genetics to identify individuals across populations?
When might evolution or plasticity allow populations to adapt to changing environmental conditions?
How do we apply theoretical tools from population and quantitative genetics to conservation problems?

title: Research
text:
Forensic genetics
We are interested in the application of population genetic, genealogical, and quantitative genetic methods to forensic settings.
Key questions

How do we best measure and convey the uncertainty associated with the probability or likelihood that a tissue sample belongs to a particular individual, population, or species?
How do genotype-by-environment interactions impact our ability to make predictions about phenotypes in a forensic setting?
How does forensic genetic genealogy perform for different genetic backgrounds and genetic marker qualities?
Can forensic genetic genealogical style approaches be used when working with zoo studbooks?



Collaborate with us
We welcome collaborations with researchers, government agencies, and conservation organizations. If you have data and questions that align with our expertise, get in touch
